methodology: Composite 0-100 = momentum 18% + commodity-alignment 18% + deep-dive 14% + analyst 13% + fundamentals 12% + news sentiment 9% + SmartScore 8% + quality 8% (missing sub-scores redistribute). High = long candidate, low = short candidate. Sources: IBKR, FMP, TipRanks, Bigdata.com (RavenPack), Carbon Arc, MetalMiner, TrueNorth.
macro: Hawkish Fed (64% Sept hike priced), strong dollar (DXY ~100.8), US-Iran conflict volatility with 60-day peace talks; metals complex broadly correcting after H1 melt-up. Q4 consensus: dollar downtrend -> possible precious rebound window in Q3/Q4.

China HC futures stabilizing/rebounding. LME still +45% YoY (producers' Q results strong). | MetalMiner: Jul MMO: 'purchase as needed' (downgraded from Jun 'buy ahead 0-3mo'); LME -15.55% Jun to $3,112/mt; drivers flipped negative (Iran resolution, Novelis Oswego return, rising USD); US A356 $2.914/lb flat, 26% of 1M range

tickers.thesis: Neo Performance Materials raised full-year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance to $140-150M from $100-110M (+38% at the midpoint, ~90% above FY2025's $76M), explicitly citing sustained higher-than-expected pricing for hafnium, gallium and rare earth elements amid tight global supply and growing supply-chain-security demand — a textbook direct confirmation of the LONG rare earths backdrop. Management notes secured hafnium/gallium/tantalum inventory and increased contracted volumes giving added confidence into H2 2026, with Q2 results due August 11.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results (Aug 11) testing the raised guidance
New Permanent Magnet facility in Europe commercial launch
Continued elevated critical-materials pricing (hafnium, gallium, REE)
risks: Guidance raise already partly priced in after the announcement
Exposure to China export-control/tariff policy on rare earth inputs
Magnequench/automotive customer concentration risk

tickers.thesis: GE Vernova is primarily an AI/grid electrification infrastructure story rather than a direct electrical-steel price play; Q1 2026 revenue grew 17% YoY to $9.34B with an EPS beat, and management raised full-year 2026 guidance (revenue to $44.5-45.5B, adjusted EBITDA margin to 12-14%, free cash flow to $6.5-7.5B) on strong Power and Electrification orders/backlog. As a buyer rather than producer of electrical steel, our sector Steel LONG bias is a mild input-cost headwind rather than a tailwind for GEV's margins, but multi-year turbine/grid order visibility and Bernstein's Outperform initiation (PT $1,206) support the name on its own AI-power-demand merits.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results July 22, 2026
Continued backlog growth in Power and Electrification segments
Grid/AI power demand driving multi-year equipment orders
risks: Premium valuation flagged by sell-side commentary
Electrical steel and other input cost inflation given Steel LONG backdrop
Execution risk on raised FY2026 guidance

evidence.note: Q1 2026 revenue $9.34B (+17% YoY), EPS beat by $1.95, FY2026 guidance raised across metrics

tickers.thesis: Howmet's Q1 2026 results beat expectations across the board — revenue +19% YoY to $2.31B, adjusted EPS $1.22, operating income +52% to $753M, EBITDA +32% to $740M (32% margin), free cash flow more than doubled to $359M — driving raised full-year guidance to $9.65B revenue and $4.94 adjusted EPS, with 18 of 23 analysts rating the stock Strong Buy and an average price target implying ~12.5% upside. This is a demand-driven (aerospace/gas-turbine engine components) rather than raw-titanium-price-driven bull case, but it benefits from the same titanium supply-chain tightness underpinning the broader materials backdrop.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results and webcast (Aug 6)
Continued aerospace OEM production ramp (narrowbody/widebody)
Further guidance raises if commercial aerospace demand holds
risks: Valuation already reflects strong Strong Buy consensus and 50.9% 52-week outperformance
Not a pure commodity-price play — more exposed to aerospace build-rate cycles than titanium spot pricing
Aerospace supply chain disruption or OEM production rate cuts

tickers.thesis: BHP's copper EBITDA contribution has more than doubled since FY23 to 51%, with production up ~30% over four years, and Berenberg raised its price target (GBP34 from GBP25) ahead of FY2026 results on Aug 18, expecting a bumper dividend. The company is actively reallocating capital toward core copper (weighing a $1.5-2B Chile desalination/energy asset sale) even as the Jansen potash project's cost rose to $6.9B with a ~$2.3B impairment and a two-year delay, illustrating the mixed non-copper portfolio drag typical of diversified miners.
catalysts: FY2026 results and fresh guidance due Aug 18, 2026
Potential $1.5-2B Chile desalination/energy asset sale to fund core copper growth
Antamina silver stream cash inflow ($4.3B) supporting balance sheet and dividend capacity
risks: Jansen potash cost overrun to $6.9B with $2.3B impairment and 2-year delay to FY2031 first production
Iron ore segment remains a drag amid China demand uncertainty
Net debt trending toward upper end of $10-20B target range

evidence.note: Copper EBITDA contribution >51% of total, more than doubled since FY23; copper production +~30% over 4 years
Berenberg raises BHP PT to GBP34, expects bumper dividend ahead of Aug 18 FY26 results
Jansen stage 2 potash cost raised to $6.9B from $4.9B, ~$2.3B impairment, first production delayed 2 years
BHP evaluating $1.5-2B sale of Chile desalination/energy assets to fund core copper business

tickers.thesis: Hudbay is executing squarely on the desk's copper LONG thesis: the Arizona Sonoran (Cactus) acquisition combined with Copper World creates what management calls the third-largest copper district in North America, with a clear path to scale annual production from ~125,000t today to 250,000t by 2030 and 350,000t longer-term. Q1 2026 results beat materially (EPS $0.40 vs $0.34 est; revenue $757M vs ~$595M YoY), and the Constancia mill capacity permit expansion extends mine life — the stock is up 108.9% over the past year, reflecting strong execution momentum.

Peru (Constancia) operational/regulatory risk
Valuation after 108.9% 1-year run

tickers.thesis: Nexa Resources posted a strong Q1 2026 (adjusted EBITDA +126% YoY to $283M, revenue +42% YoY to $888M, zinc production +18% YoY with a record Aripuanã quarter), and despite a May 13 Cajamarquilla smelter fire that will cost ~7,000 tonnes (2% of annual output) of refined zinc, the company maintained full-year 2026 zinc production guidance (+6% at the midpoint) with recovery expected in H2. Management also flagged a constructive zinc market structure - low exchange inventories, easing mine supply, recovering treatment charges - reinforcing the book's LONG zinc stance, though the stock's 164% one-year rally means much of the good news may already be reflected in price.
catalysts: 2026 zinc production guidance maintained (+6% YoY at midpoint) despite Cajamarquilla disruption
Record Aripuanã mine output driving 18% YoY zinc production growth
Favorable zinc market structure: low exchange inventories, easing mine supply, recovering treatment charges
risks: Cajamarquilla smelter fire cut ~7,000t refined zinc (2% of annual output), weighing on Q2
Execution risk on H2 2026 production recovery at Cajamarquilla
Stock +164% over the past year; forward P/E of 5.02x is cheap vs. industry 13.66x but sentiment already very positive

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA +126% YoY to $283M; zinc production +18% YoY; revenue +42% YoY to $888M; record Aripuanã output
Cajamarquilla fire cuts ~7,000t refined zinc (2% of annual output); FY26 zinc guidance maintained, +6% YoY at midpoint; forward P/E 5.02x vs industry 13.66x
Zinc market to benefit from easing mine supply, recovering treatment charges, low exchange inventories; copper market structurally tight too

tickers.thesis: Vedanta's Q1 FY27 production release (Jul 3 2026) reported record first-quarter mined metal output at Zinc India (268,000t, +1% YoY, highest-ever Q1) with saleable metal +4% and refined zinc +6%, alongside copper sales at an eight-year Q1 high (+3%) - a direct, current-quarter validation of the zinc/tin LONG call. The company completed a four-way demerger (Vedanta Aluminium, Power, Oil&Gas, Iron&Steel spun out, Jun 15 2026), leaving the remaining Vedanta Limited (VEDL) more concentrated in zinc/base metals; Gamsberg Phase 2 is targeted to add production from July 2026. As an India-listed name (NSE/BSE), IBKR tradability is a real constraint - flag this for execution before treating it as an actionable position.
catalysts: Gamsberg Phase 2 ramp (targeted Jul 2026)
Post-demerger re-rating as a more focused zinc/base-metals entity
Continued zinc/copper price strength
risks: IBKR/foreign-listing tradability - VEDL is NSE/BSE-listed India equity with no current US ADR
Demerger execution/value-leakage risk across four new listed entities
India market volatility

evidence.note: Record Q1 FY27 Zinc India mined metal output (268,000t); copper sales +3% to 8-year Q1 high; Gamsberg Phase 2 targeted Jul 2026
Vedanta demerger completed; Vedanta Aluminium, Power, Oil&Gas, Iron&Steel listed separately, VEDL retains zinc/base metals concentration

analyst.BMI: Expects elevated tin prices through 2026 with possible H2 moderation

tickers.thesis: PT Timah's Q1 2026 refined tin production surged 81.9% YoY to 5,630 tonnes, marking a major operational turnaround after 2024-2025 disruptions from an illegal-mining crackdown and corruption-related scrutiny, with Indonesian tin exports now normalizing (as of May 2026) and management explicitly pivoting focus from volume to profitability. BMI expects tin prices to stay elevated through 2026 on continued supply tightness (Myanmar, Indonesia), though flags possible H2 moderation as supply issues ease -- overall confirming the tin-long thesis with a credible recovery narrative, tempered by a note of caution on the back half.
catalysts: Continued production recovery toward pre-crackdown levels
New 2026-2028 labor/collective agreement reducing labor disruption risk
Management's explicit pivot to profitability-focused strategy
risks: BMI flags possible H2 2026 tin price moderation as supply issues ease
Execution risk in translating production recovery into sustained profitability
Indonesian regulatory/mining policy risk given recent corruption scrutiny

evidence.note: Q1 2026 refined tin production +81.9% YoY to 5,630 tonnes; Indonesian exports normalizing as of May 2026 after 2024-2025 crackdown-driven declines; BMI sees elevated 2026 prices with possible H2 moderation.
New 2026-2028 collective labor agreement signed; management pivoting focus from production volume to profitability.

tickers.thesis: Tata Steel posted FY2025-26 consolidated EBITDA of INR34,848 crore (+35% YoY) with margins expanding 320bps to 15%, record deliveries, INR10,738 crore free cash flow, and dual investment-grade ratings, while commissioning Kalinganagar Phase II to reach 26.1 MTPA capacity en route to a 40 MTPA long-term target — consistent with the LONG steel backdrop. The main offsetting risk is a Competition Commission of India probe into 32 steel producers (including Tata Steel) over alleged HRC/CRC/TMT price coordination, which the company disputes but which adds regulatory overhang.
catalysts: Continued Kalinganagar and NINL capacity ramp-up toward 40 MTPA
Value-added/downstream (Tubes, Tinplate, Wires) mix shift supporting margins
US HRC price tightness (tariff-protected) supporting realizations
risks: CCI antitrust investigation into HRC/CRC/TMT pricing across 32 Indian steelmakers
China steel oversupply/weak demand capping global price ceiling
NSE-listed; foreign-ordinary-share access via IBKR may be limited, GDR/ADR alternative should be checked

evidence.note: Kalinganagar Phase II commissioned, capacity to 26.1 MTPA, path to 40 MTPA; INR4/share FY26 dividend recommended

analyst.CFRA: Buy, $56 12-month PT (2026 EPS raised to $4.04, 2027 to $4.63)

tickers.thesis: Despite CFRA raising 2026/2027 EPS estimates and Barrick posting a 195% YoY surge in Q1 FCF to $1.21B on maintained production guidance (2.90-3.25Moz gold, 190-220kt copper), spot gold has fallen roughly 26% from its January 2026 peak above $5,500/oz to the $4,100-4,150 range by early July -- its steepest multi-quarter decline since 2013 -- pressuring Barrick shares even as fundamentals remain solid. This confirms the short-medium thesis: strong operating leverage cuts both ways, and price momentum has decisively broken down.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results Aug 10, 2026
Planned year-end North American IPO
Lumwana expansion (Q1 2028 first production) and Fourmile Tier-One potential
risks: Further gold price downside on Fed policy/rate path
Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran) removing safe-haven bid
High operating leverage amplifies downside to earnings if price weakness persists

evidence.note: PT $56; Q1 FCF +195% YoY to $1.21B; production guidance maintained; $2.4B net cash.
Gold down ~26% from Jan 2026 peak (>$5,500/oz) to $4,100-4,150/oz range; Barrick shares fell 1.8% pre-market on the decline.

tickers.thesis: Materion is a specialty materials supplier to semiconductors/aerospace/defense/energy, not a pure LME-aluminum play, so it largely sidesteps our sector-level Aluminum SHORT bias. Near-term catalyst is the July 9 New Ideas Summer Investor Conference presentation; customer concentration in semis/aerospace/defense remains the key risk.

tickers.thesis: Rio Tinto is reducing its legacy iron-ore over-dependence and benefiting from resilient iron ore, copper and aluminium prices, with a $5-10B portfolio simplification program underway (TiO2/Borates sale, $650M opex savings already booked) and Oyu Tolgoi copper shipments restored after a brief Mongolia blockade. However, the picture is genuinely mixed for this diversified miner: Berenberg expects H1 earnings to miss consensus on a revised commodity deck, and RBC downgraded to Underperform, arguing upside is hard to see outside aluminium (28% of EBITDA) with iron ore pricing already assuming $102/t and vulnerable to Chinese economic softness.
catalysts: Portfolio simplification targeting $5-10B unlocked value, $650M opex savings already achieved
TiO2/Borates business sale process underway plus potential by-product streaming
Oyu Tolgoi copper shipments normalized after Mongolia road blockade
risks: Berenberg models H1 revenue/EPS below consensus ($30.2B vs $30.8B; EPS $3.87 vs $4.19)
RBC downgrade to Underperform citing limited upside ex-aluminium and stretched valuation (5.5x on spot)
Iron ore pricing already assumes $102/t; Chinese demand softness is a key swing risk

evidence.note: AlphaValue/Baader raises PT, notes Rio reducing iron ore over-dependence, right commodity mix, EPS forecast raised 38.5% for 2026
Berenberg expects H1 earnings miss on revised commodity deck; notes resilient iron ore/copper/aluminium prices YTD
RBC downgrades to Underperform, sees limited upside outside aluminium, iron ore priced at $102/t, prefers Glencore
Oyu Tolgoi copper concentrate shipments resumed after brief blockade interruption

tickers.thesis: Franco-Nevada's royalty/streaming model continues to outperform on record gold prices — Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $2.38 beat consensus ($2.11) on revenue of $650.7M (up from $368.4M YoY), and CIBC named it its top pick for precious metals exposure with a C$480 target after its Ontario mine tour. Consistent with the desk's view that royalty companies score higher than operators even as gold is SHORT-medium overall, FNV's insulated cost structure (revenues largely unaffected by input cost inflation) and 2026 GEO guidance (510-570koz) support a defensive-but-not-aggressive stance.
catalysts: Cobre Panama stockpile-ore processing approval adding incremental GEO deliveries (mostly 2027)
Ontario portfolio growth (Porcupine/Cote/Detour Lake/Island Gold) highlighted on CIBC mine tour
Continued M&A/deal pipeline deployment in Tier-1 jurisdictions
risks: Gold price is the desk's SHORT-medium call; a pullback compresses royalty NAV even without operating leverage
Cobre Panama GEO contribution remains 'relatively moderate' in 2026, back-half loaded
New independent chair transition (Tom Albanese) is a governance watch item

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted EPS $2.38 vs $1.07 YoY, beat consensus $2.11; revenue $650.7M vs $368.4M YoY
CIBC reiterates Outperform, PT C$480, calls FNV 'top pick for precious metals exposure'
2026 GEO sales guidance maintained at 510,000-570,000 oz, excluding Cobre Panama upside

tickers.thesis: Yunnan Tin is a strong direct play on the desk's Zinc/Tin LONG (tin bullish) thesis, benefiting from both tin price strength and an emerging AI-driven indium demand story (indium phosphide substrate prices surged from $800 to $2,500 within a year); 2025 revenue rose 3.7% to RMB43.5bn with net profit up 36.1%, and growth accelerated further in Q1 2026. Dongwu Securities explicitly recommends the tin sector on supply constraints, though the company's structural weakness ('large-scale smelting, small-scale mining') means it relies heavily on externally purchased raw material, pressuring operating cash flow via higher prepayments and margin deposits.
catalysts: AI/data-center-driven indium phosphide demand (indium demand forecast to grow from 19t in 2025 to 419t by 2030)
Sector-wide tin price strength on supply constraints (Dongwu Securities 'spring of tin, indium, hafnium' thesis)
Q1 2026 revenue/profit growth accelerating vs. FY2025
risks: Structural reliance on externally purchased ore ('large-scale smelting, small-scale mining') pressures cash flow via prepayments/margin deposits
2025 operating cash flow fell 62% YoY despite profit growth, a working-capital red flag
China smelting capacity concentration risk

evidence.note: 2025 revenue RMB43.535bn (+3.7% YoY), net profit RMB1.966bn (+36.1% YoY); Q1 2026 growth accelerated further; operating cash flow fell 62% YoY on higher prepayments/margin deposits

Dongwu Securities recommends tin sector (incl. Yunnan Tin) citing supply-side constraints expected to keep mid-price of tin rising; indium phosphide demand for AI data centers to drive indium demand growth >20%

tickers.thesis: Malaysia Smelting delivered a record quarter: Q1 FY26 net profit surged more than fivefold YoY to RM42.93M on revenue +23.6% to RM457.04M, driven by tin prices up 32% YTD to ~US$53,900/tonne on the LME -- closely matching the backdrop's $53,450 reference level -- and the board declared both a final and special dividend. Public Investment Bank's outlook remains positive on continued supply deficit (Myanmar, Indonesia disruptions) keeping prices elevated, directly confirming the tin-long thesis.
catalysts: Continued tin supply deficit from Myanmar/Indonesia disruptions
MSC scaling mining investment to capture higher volumes at elevated prices
Potential rare-earth optionality given MSC's mining infrastructure
risks: Tin price pullback if Myanmar/Indonesia supply normalizes faster than expected
Margin sensitivity to LME price swings given smelting cost structure

evidence.note: Q1 FY26 net profit up ~5.5x YoY to RM42.93M; tin prices +32% YTD to ~US$53,900/tonne LME.
Tin prices US$49,000-56,000/tonne LME as of May 2026; supply concerns in Myanmar/Indonesia plus energy/digital transition demand sustaining elevated prices.

tickers.thesis: Nippon Steel Vice Chairman Takahiro Mori told Reuters (Jun 18 2026) that US Steel, acquired for $14.9B in June 2025, is set for over 100 billion yen in profit this year and 300-400 billion yen long-term annual earnings, driven by steady demand and import tariffs - directly validating the steel LONG thesis (US HRC ~$1,124/st, tariff-tight). JBIC's financing exposure to the US Steel deal ($3.7B loan) and the Sanyo Special Steel merger (May 2026) reinforce a company actively consolidating and leaning into US-market steel exposure, though China steel weakness and Brazil/Usiminas import competition are an offsetting drag noted in Ternium's July 8 6-K.
catalysts: US Steel earnings ramp toward 300-400bn yen annual run-rate
Sanyo Special Steel merger synergies
US tariff-driven HRC price ceiling
risks: China steel weak demand dragging global pricing
Brazil/LatAm import competition (Usiminas)
Yen/FX and integration risk post-US Steel deal

tickers.thesis: Boliden's Garpenberg mine remains disrupted by a March 2026 seismic event affecting the Lappberget orebody (~70% of near-term planned volumes), creating near-term earnings uncertainty, but Berenberg turned 'more constructive' on the recovery (PT raised to SEK670) citing better-than-expected medium-term volume guidance and Q2 output guided at 0.1mt, while AlphaValue/Baader separately raised its PT to SEK706, framing Boliden as a 'reliable' European base/precious-metals proxy with ~96% of sales from the region — consistent with the LONG zinc/tin backdrop and limited direct exposure to Middle East disruption. Multiple brokers see the recent sell-off as an attractive entry point rather than a structural impairment.
catalysts: Garpenberg rehabilitation progress and volume guidance updates
Continued strength in zinc/tin prices feeding through to realized margins
Byproduct precious-metals and sulphuric-acid exposure as an upside kicker
risks: Garpenberg orebody disruption could prove more persistent than currently modeled
EPS estimates were cut ~12% for 2026 even as PT was raised
Stockholm-listed; confirm IBKR access/liquidity for BOL.ST

evidence.note: AlphaValue/Baader raised PT to SEK706, calling Boliden a reliable European metals proxy amid critical-minerals supply chain focus
AlphaValue/Baader called recent sell-off an attractive entry point; cut 2026 EPS ~12% on Garpenberg but reiterated positive rating
Berenberg raised PT to SEK670, 'more constructive' on Garpenberg recovery, better-than-expected medium-term volume guidance

tickers.thesis: Metallus is a clean read-through on the desk's Steel LONG thesis: Section 232 tariffs continue to insulate its primary steel products (April 2026 tariff updates hit only downstream derivatives, not Metallus' core products), order book grew YoY on industrial/defense demand and onshoring, and the company has pushed through bar/tube price actions ($120/ton and $100/ton respectively) while new bloom reheat and roller furnace capacity comes online through Q3 2026. Simply Wall St flags the tariff-support/onshoring narrative but also notes cyclical end-market and leverage risk via its asset-backed credit facility.
catalysts: New bloom reheat furnace (150 tons/hr vs 100 legacy) fully operational early-to-mid Q3 2026; roller furnace late Q3
Bar/tube price actions flowing through non-contract volume (~30% of annual volume) in H2 2026
Continued Section 232 tariff protection on primary steel products
risks: Heavy reliance on cyclical industrial end markets
High fixed costs and leverage via asset-backed credit facility
Exposure to tariffs on certain imported inputs despite core-product protection

evidence.note: Q1 2026 earnings call: shipments +11% sequentially, order book grew YoY, Section 232 tariffs continue to support competitive position
Metallus fits US onshoring story as domestic steel producer benefiting from tariffs making imports less competitive; Q1 2026 higher sales/earnings plus buybacks

tickers.thesis: Morgan Stanley cut Vale on July 8, citing weaker iron ore prices from expanding global supply surpluses, softer Chinese steel production, and rising production costs, now expecting 2026/27 earnings below consensus - despite 'continued progress' in the base metals (copper) business, which the same note flags as MS's preferred sector-wide commodity. Vale's nickel segment remains highly price-sensitive (EBITDA could range from ~$1.15B to ~$2.0B in 2026 depending on whether nickel trades $16k-$20k/t), consistent with this book's neutral-to-bearish nickel stance. Net: iron ore drag currently dominates the diversified-miner copper tailwind for Vale specifically, ahead of Q2 2026 production/sales (Jul 21) and earnings (Jul 30) reports.
catalysts: Q2 2026 production/sales report July 21, financial results July 30, 2026
Continued progress in base metals/copper business per Morgan Stanley
Nickel segment has meaningful upside leverage if prices rally toward $20k/t
risks: Morgan Stanley downgrade/estimate cut on weaker iron ore prices and supply surplus
Softer Chinese steel production and rising production costs pressuring 2026/27 EPS below consensus
Iron ore remains Vale's dominant revenue driver despite copper/nickel diversification

evidence.note: Morgan Stanley downgrades Vale on weaker iron ore prices, supply surplus, softer Chinese steel, rising costs; 2026/27 EPS below consensus
Nickel segment EBITDA sensitivity: ~$1,150-$2,000M in 2026 depending on nickel price ($16k-$20k/t)
Q2 2026 production/sales report Jul 21; financial results Jul 30; conference call Jul 31

'Peak order' concerns among investors that Q3 results need to reassure against
Electrical steel input cost inflation could pressure Grid Technologies margins

tickers.thesis: SSR Mining is executing a clear portfolio-simplification and capital-return story: it agreed to exit its Hod Maden stake in Turkey for a 4.0% NSR royalty (RBC sector-perform, $45 PT), announced an additional $500 million buyback plus reinstatement of a quarterly dividend on top of $774 million already returned since 2021, and the $1.5 billion Copler mine sale remains on track to close by Q3 2026, all while guiding to 450,000-535,000 gold-equivalent ounces for 2026 as an Americas-focused producer. The combination of de-risking (full Turkey exit), a ~5% year-to-date shareholder return yield, and a positive Earnings ESP heading into the August 4 print supports a constructive near-term setup, though the NSR-for-equity swap modestly trims fair value versus the prior ownership stake.
catalysts: Copler mine sale ($1.5B) closing by Q3 2026

tickers.thesis: As China's dominant light rare earth (Baotou) producer, Northern Rare Earth posted Q1 2026 revenue of RMB11.86B, up 27.69% YoY, and set its Q3 2026 rare earth concentrate transaction price at RMB38,565/tonne — a modest 0.62% sequential decline from Q2's RMB38,800/tonne but still reflecting the structurally elevated pricing consistent with the LONG rare earths backdrop (NdPr +74% YoY, supply gap). The company's quasi-monopoly position on China's concentrate pricing mechanism gives it direct, near-monopolistic leverage to the rare earth price complex.
catalysts: Continued NdPr/rare earth oxide price strength feeding through to quarterly concentrate pricing resets
Chinese export-quota policy tightening supply further
Downstream new-material and magnet business growth
risks: Sequential (QoQ) concentrate price softness (-0.62% in Q3) bears watching for trend reversal
State-directed pricing mechanism reduces pure market-price leverage
Shanghai A-share listing; confirm IBKR/QFII access — foreign tradability is limited without Stock Connect/appropriate account

Combined with the broader silver price pullback noted alongside gold's decline, this supports a cautious, SHORT-low stance rather than chasing the name here.

tickers.thesis: REalloys is expanding commercial neodymium-praseodymium output to ~525 tons/yr (targeting Q4 2027 for dysprosium/terbium oxides) and closed a $100M private placement (Jun 26 2026) to fund it, directly levered to the rare earths LONG call (NdPr +74% YoY) as a North American ex-China supply-chain play. However, the Q1 2026 10-Q explicitly carries a going-concern qualification from its auditor and negative operating cash flows, and the company's own filing flags NdPr price volatility as a material risk to margins - this is a real but early-stage, financing-dependent bet on the rare earths re-rating catching up to spot NdPr.
catalysts: Saskatchewan Research Council facility upgrade completion
Dysprosium/terbium oxide production start (targeted Q4 2027)
Heavy rare earth metallization plant construction
risks: Going-concern doubt / negative operating cash flow (per auditor, Q1 2026 10-Q)
NdPr/rare earth price volatility and China export-policy swings
Dilution risk from continued equity financing

Closed $100M private placement at $14.25/share

tickers.thesis: Euro Manganese's Chvaletice project PEA confirms a base-case post-tax NPV of $492M (13.8% IRR) at an HPMSM price of $2,888/t, with upside to $798M NPV if prices reach $3,275/t, but the company just amended its Orion facility to convert debt into a royalty (2.29-2.46% of project revenue) to shore up its balance sheet ahead of financing conditions that still require securing offtakes for 40% of first-five-year production and a strategic investor. This is a pre-revenue optionality name whose near-term catalysts are financing milestones rather than manganese price moves, consistent with a neutral manganese macro stance.
catalysts: Chvaletice PEA base case NPV $492M / IRR 13.8% (upside case $798M at higher HPMSM prices)
Orion facility amendment converts debt to royalty, easing near-term balance sheet pressure
Potential offtake agreements (40% of first 5-year production) and strategic investor could unlock project financing
risks: Pre-production, pre-revenue stage; project financing still contingent on offtakes and a strategic investor
Cumulative royalty stack (up to ~4.75% combined across facilities) dilutes long-run project economics
Manganese price backdrop rated neutral, limited near-term commodity catalyst

evidence.note: Euro Manganese amends Orion facility, converts outstanding loan to a 2.29-2.46% revenue royalty pending fundraise condition
Chvaletice PEA base case: post-tax NPV $492M, IRR 13.8% at HPMSM $2,888/t; upside case NPV $798M at $3,275/t
Key financing condition: offtake agreements for 40% of first 5 years' production plus a strategic investor required for $30M tranche drawdown

tickers.thesis: Agnico disclosed a rock mass movement at the Barnat open pit (Canadian Malartic complex) that will cut full-year 2026 gold production by 60,000-80,000oz in H2 (guidance now expected near the low end of 3.3-3.5Moz) with further 2027-2028 impacts of up to ~150,000oz/year, even though Q2 production of ~845,000oz was slightly ahead of plan; shares are already down 5.5% over the past month, underperforming both the S&P 500 and Basic Materials sector. As one of the lower-cost, longer-life major producers, AEM fits the 'low-cost defensive' pocket of the gold SHORT-medium backdrop, but the near-term guidance cut and softer gold tape argue for a neutral rather than outright bullish stance ahead of the July 29 Q2 report.

Resolution/mitigation plan for Barnat geotechnical issue
Canadian Malartic complex progressing toward 1Moz/year in early 2030s

Gold backdrop is SHORT-medium on hawkish Fed, -14% 3M
Geotechnical/seismic risk also present at LaRonde, Goldex, Fosterville

tickers.thesis: Novelis' FY2026 10-K shows net income cratering 98% YoY to $15M (from $683M) and Adjusted EBITDA down 9% to $1,645M, driven by the Oswego plant fires, weaker recycled-metal benefit, and higher net tariffs, with adjusted free cash flow swinging to a $2.4B outflow. As a fabricator (Hindalco subsidiary) exposed to LME aluminum "metal price lag" and regional premium volatility rather than upside commodity beta, this confirms the packaging/fabricator short thesis on tariff and cost pass-through drag.

Recovery from Oswego fire disruption
Tariff/regional premium normalization
risks: Further tariff escalation compressing margins
Metal price lag timing mismatches
Parent Hindalco capital allocation priorities

tickers.thesis: Ivanhoe fits the Copper LONG thesis on a recovery arc: Kamoa-Kakula copper output is set to rise in H2 2026 as mining rates increase and inventories are drawn down, positioning it as a key source of global copper supply growth into a tighter 2026 market. However, this follows an April 2026 cut to 2026/2027 output guidance after seismic disruption at Kakula, and revised cash cost guidance ($2.60-3.00/lb for 2026) reflects the lower-production, higher-cost base — so the thesis is a rebound off a reset floor rather than unambiguous strength.
catalysts: H2 2026 ramp in Kamoa-Kakula mining rates and inventory drawdown
Medium-term cash cost target of ~$2.00/lb from 2028 as smelter efficiencies mature
Global copper market tightening into 2026 per analyst forecasts
risks: April 2026 guidance cut for 2026/2027 output following seismic disruption at Kakula
Elevated 2026/2027 cash costs ($2.60-3.00/lb) vs. medium-term target
Execution risk on capex deferral and mill utilization ramp

evidence.note: Ivanhoe says Kamoa-Kakula copper output set to rise in H2 2026; company cut 2026/2027 output forecasts in April after seismic disruption at Kakula
2026/2027 cash cost guidance revised to $2.60-3.00/lb (2026) reflecting lower production forecast and grade; medium-term target ~$2.00/lb from 2028

tickers.thesis: Jinduicheng Molybdenum is riding a sharp molybdenum price rally (oxide/ferromolybdenum up >30% YTD 2026, Shanghai spot nearly doubling from RMB 464/t in January to RMB 591/t in June), which drove Q1 2026 revenue up 26.7% YoY and net profit up 33.0% YoY. However, the stock has already more than doubled YTD and its price-to-book ratio just hit a decade high after a string of limit-up days before pulling back, suggesting much of the 'computing power metal' rally is priced in; as a Shanghai A-share it also carries meaningful IBKR/foreign-investor accessibility constraints (Stock Connect/QFII).
catalysts: Molybdenum oxide/ferromolybdenum prices up >30% YTD 2026, direct earnings pass-through
Q1 2026 revenue +26.7% YoY, net profit +33.0% YoY
Broader China 'computing power metal' sector rally sentiment (minor metals/rare earths)
risks: Stock +100%+ YTD with PB at a decade high; valuation has run ahead of fundamentals
China A-share listing limits IBKR/foreign investor access (Stock Connect/QFII required)
Molybdenum backdrop rated neutral by the book - rally may be overextended

tickers.thesis: Hindustan Zinc posted a fifth straight year of record Q1 mined metal output (+1% YoY to 268,000t, zinc output +6% YoY to 213,000t) and Q4 FY26 net profit rose 67.6% YoY, supporting the desk's zinc LONG thesis on strong underlying production. However, the stock fell 16% in June — its second-worst month of 2026 — driven primarily by a sustained drop in global silver prices (silver contributes an estimated 45% of profitability) plus a governance overhang from a potential government stake sale and an ED/FEMA investigation into parent Vedanta, muting the read-through purely to zinc fundamentals.
catalysts: 510,000 tpa fertiliser plant and hot acid leaching tech completion (Q2 FY26-27)
Continued record mined metal output trend
Chanderiya smelter Zinc Mark/Copper Mark sustainability certification
risks: High sensitivity to silver price (~45% of profitability), not pure zinc exposure
Government stake-sale overhang and ED/FEMA investigation into Vedanta
Foreign India NSE listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: Q4 FY26 net profit +67.6% YoY to INR5,033cr; shares fell 16% in June on silver price weakness; govt considering further stake sale
Q1 FY26-27 mined metal output +1% YoY to 268,000t (5th straight record quarter); zinc output +6% YoY to 213,000t; FY25-26 record annual output 1,114,000t

tickers.thesis: Nornickel itself forecasts a global nickel market surplus of ~20,000 tonnes in 2026 and ~55,000 tonnes in 2027, consistent with the neutral-to-bearish nickel view, while LME nickel fell to $17,230/ton in the window. The board recommended skipping the 2025 dividend to preserve financial stability amid 'elevated macroeconomic instability,' and though not under direct sanctions, Western measures are pushing buyers away from Russian metal and forcing sales redirection to Asia. CRITICAL: GMKN.ME trades on MOEX (Moscow Exchange) and is not tradable through IBKR given Russia sanctions exposure -- this is a research-only/non-actionable name regardless of the fundamental view.
catalysts: Potential 2026 dividend resumption per CEO commentary
Copper concentrate deficit / zero benchmark TCs for 2026 (positive for copper segment)
risks: NOT tradable via IBKR (sanctioned/foreign-exchange listing)
IBKR/Western brokerage tradability -- MOEX-listed, effectively inaccessible to US/EU investors under sanctions regime
Nickel market surplus forecast by the company itself
Further Western sanctions escalation on Russian metals trade

evidence.note: Nornickel forecasts global nickel surplus of ~20,000t in 2026 and ~55,000t in 2027; LME nickel fell to $17,230/ton.
Board recommends no 2025 dividend citing macro instability; EBITDA +9% to $5.7B; not under direct sanctions but Western measures reduce Western buyer appetite for Russian metal.
Nornickel forecasts copper concentrate deficit and zero benchmark treatment charges for 2026.

tickers.thesis: Avalon sits on a strategically significant North American rare earth resource (Nechalacho) and is advancing a PEA update to its 2013 DFS, which aligns directionally with the desk's Rare Earths LONG thesis (NdPr +74% YoY). However, the dominant recent news is a 180:1 share consolidation completed July 8, 2026 after shares fell to $0.06 — a balance-sheet/capital-structure distress signal ahead of a potential US listing bid, not a fundamentals-driven catalyst, so this remains a pre-revenue, dilution-risk story rather than a direct way to play RE price strength.
catalysts: 180:1 share consolidation completed, intended to support a future US exchange listing
Nechalacho PEA update advancing, updating the 2013 DFS on North America's most strategically significant RE resource
Sector tailwind from NdPr price strength (+74% YoY per desk backdrop)
risks: 180:1 reverse split reflects severe share price deterioration (traded at $0.06 pre-consolidation) and dilution history
Pre-revenue development stage; no production or near-term earnings to capture RE price upside
Contingent on future US capital markets access, not yet secured

evidence.note: Avalon completed 180:1 common share consolidation, effective July 8, 2026, to support potential future US exchange listing
Avalon provides YTD progress update on Nechalacho Rare Earth Elements and Zirconium Project, advancing PEA update to 2013 DFS

tickers.thesis: Minsur benefits from the desk's tin-bullish thesis (LME tin ~$53,450) even as its own Q1 2026 refined tin output from the Pisco smelter fell 2.9% YoY — supply softness at Minsur plus disruptions at Renison (Tasmania, -13% QoQ) and the DRC's Bisie mine reinforces low global tin stocks and price support, with BMI/Sucden Financial noting the market stays vulnerable to renewed squeezes into H2 2026 on Indonesia/Myanmar supply risk. IMPORTANT: MINSURI1 trades on the Lima Stock Exchange and is very likely NOT tradable via IBKR — flag this as an execution/tradability constraint before sizing any position.
catalysts: Global tin stocks low and declining on LME/SHFE, keeping the market exposed to squeezes
Semiconductor-industry demand expected to keep tin prices elevated through 2026
Minsur's RDP copper-gold exploration investment (Pacific Ridge, BC) diversifies beyond tin
risks: NOT tradable via IBKR (sanctioned/foreign-exchange listing)
TRADABILITY: Lima-listed (MINSURI1) — likely not accessible via IBKR; confirm broker access before any trade
Minsur's own Pisco smelter output declined 2.9% YoY in Q1 2026, an idiosyncratic supply miss even as prices firm
Indonesia/Myanmar supply risk cuts both ways — could also ease tightness if resolved

evidence.note: Minsur Q1 2026 refined tin output from Pisco smelter down 2.9% YoY to 8,314 tonnes; global tin stocks low on LME/SHFE, market vulnerable to squeezes into H2 2026
Minsur invests in Pacific Ridge Exploration's RDP copper-gold project in British Columbia, diversifying beyond core tin business

tickers.thesis: Moody's upgraded Volcan's ratings to B1 from B2 (outlook stable), citing improved cash-flow visibility from the June 2026 ramp-up of the Romina polymetallic project (expected to grow zinc and silver production 26% and 10% respectively over 2026-2028 at a lower ~$50/MT cost versus the 2025 consolidated $54.3/MT), $280M cash on hand, targeted $60M of 2026 cost reductions, and no major debt maturities before 2032; Fitch separately estimates a 10% rise in zinc/silver prices would add $65-80M to 2026 EBITDA, directly levering the LONG zinc backdrop. The company remains a top-10 global zinc producer, though scale, geographic concentration in Peru, and a third-quartile cost position are structural constraints.
catalysts: Romina ramp-up driving zinc/silver volume growth through 2028
Targeted $60M (2026) and $20M (2027) cost reductions toward $57/MT unit cost
Further credit rating upgrades on improved FCF
risks: NOT tradable via IBKR (sanctioned/foreign-exchange listing)
Single-country (Peru) concentration and modest scale versus global peers
Third-quartile cost position versus peer average
Lima-listed; foreign listing likely has very limited or no IBKR tradability — verify ADR/OTC alternative before considering exposure

evidence.note: Production expected to recover to ~280,000 MT in 2026; 10% zinc/silver price rise adds $65-80M to 2026 EBITDA
Romina ramp-up (June 2026) to cut unit costs to ~$50/MT; targeted $60M cost reduction in 2026
Moody's upgraded Volcan to B1 from B2, outlook stable, on improved cash flow visibility and $280M cash position

tickers.thesis: Outokumpu's Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA improved sharply to €65M from €10M in Q4 2025, driven by 27% higher stainless deliveries and stronger US pricing, with Q2 guidance calling for further delivery growth (0-10% QoQ) and higher EBITDA — a more constructive trajectory than the desk's nickel/stainless NEUTRAL-bearish default. EU steel safeguard measures effective July 1, 2026 and CBAM-driven import substitution (cold-rolled stainless imports to EU nearly halved) provide a structural tailwind, alongside a €100M cost-savings restructuring program targeted by 2027.
catalysts: EU steel safeguard measures (July 1, 2026) and CBAM import substitution
Restructuring program (€100M annual savings by 2027)
New Hampshire low-CO2 ferrochrome pilot plant (H1 2027)
risks: Middle East conflict raising freight/energy costs if prolonged
Supply-chain implementation backlog still weighing on results
Foreign Finland listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA €65M (vs €10M Q4 2025); stainless deliveries +27% QoQ; EU steel safeguards effective July 1, 2026

tickers.thesis: South32 agreed to sell its bauxite/alumina/aluminum operations to Alcoa for ~$4.1B upfront cash-and-stock plus a CVR worth up to $750M tied to future alumina/aluminum prices, exiting a segment squarely in the SHORT aluminum backdrop and crystallizing value at a premium; separately South32 is set to gain US Forest Service approval for its $2B Arizona zinc-manganese mine (Hermosa) with full production targeted by 2029, shifting the remaining portfolio toward the LONG zinc trade. Divestiture proceeds plus a growth project in a bullish commodity is a favorable re-rating catalyst, though close isn't until H1 2027.
catalysts: Alcoa deal shareholder/regulatory approval and close (H1 2027)
US Forest Service approval for Hermosa Arizona zinc-manganese mine
CVR payments if alumina/aluminum prices exceed strike prices
risks: Long ~1-year wait to closing introduces deal risk
CVR payout is contingent on higher alumina/aluminum prices, which are currently under pressure
ASX/LSE/JSE multi-listing complicates IBKR tradability for US accounts

evidence.note: Alcoa to acquire South32 bauxite/alumina/aluminum assets for $4.1B cash+stock, ~$900M NPV synergies expected
South32 set to gain US approval for $2B Arizona zinc/manganese mine, full production targeted 2029
Up to $750M CVR tied to alumina/aluminum prices exceeding strike prices over four annual periods; close expected H1 2027
Deal expected to lift Alcoa production capacity to 3.2Mt aluminum/14.8Mt alumina, EPS +85% YoY to $8.09 in 2026

tickers.thesis: Gold Fields delivered a strong operational quarter (Q1 2026 production +15% YoY, guidance reaffirmed) with an 8% five-year production CAGR pipeline and trades at a valuation discount to peers (5.0x EV/EBITDA vs. 6x peer average), and analysts including Canaccord (upgraded to Buy) and JPMorgan (Overweight) stayed constructive even as gold pulled back from highs. Despite genuinely strong fundamentals, the book's short-medium gold macro view caps enthusiasm: the name is a pure-play on a metal we expect to cool from the current $4,100+ tape.
catalysts: 8% five-year production CAGR with Windfall (Tier-1) project nearing construction
Q1 2026 production +15% YoY, full-year guidance reaffirmed
Valuation discount to gold-miner peer group on EV/EBITDA and FCF yield
risks: Gold price backdrop rated short-medium despite current $4,100+ spot strength
Single-commodity concentration to a gold price reversal
African/South African operating jurisdiction and cost inflation risk

tickers.thesis: Aperam guided Q2 2026 adjusted EBITDA 'significantly higher' than Q1's EUR90m (consensus EUR121m), helped by EU trade defense measures taking effect July 1 (reducing import competition and lifting European mill utilization) and Brazil's return to full seasonal strength, plus continued strength in Alloys & Specialities (LNG, aerospace, electrical). But management has flagged 'low' European stainless demand for three consecutive quarters with no change, and the sequential improvement is being driven by trade barriers and seasonality rather than a genuine nickel/stainless price recovery - consistent with the book's neutral-to-bearish nickel/stainless stance.
catalysts: EU trade defense measures effective July 1, 2026 lifting European mill capacity utilization
Brazil returning to full seasonal strength in Q2 2026
Alloys & Specialities order book strength in LNG, electrical/electronics, aerospace
risks: European stainless demand still described as 'low'/challenging for 3 straight quarters
Improvement driven by trade protection/seasonality, not underlying nickel/stainless price recovery
Technical picture shows bearish short-term moving average signals

evidence.note: Q2 2026 adjusted EBITDA guided significantly higher than Q1's EUR90m; consensus EUR121m; trade defense and Brazil seasonality cited as drivers
Aperam confirms 'significant' sequential EBITDA increase in Q2 2026, driven by trade defense and Brazil seasonal return
Technical analysis shows neutral-to-bearish trend with bearish short-term moving average signals

tickers.thesis: Manganese spot prices spiked toward US$5.16/dmtu CIF (Fastmarkets semi-carbonate index) on Middle East supply disruption, lifting Tshipi's annualized EBITDA run-rate above A$120M and supporting Jupiter's debt-free, high-payout structure (historically ~84% of share price returned via dividends). However, underlying earnings have declined ~10% annually over five years and recent insider selling was flagged, while a speculative Exxaro takeover premium discussion remains forum-sourced and unconfirmed -- consistent with a neutral stance where near-term price tailwinds are offset by weak longer-run earnings trend and unclear M&A catalyst.
catalysts: Sustained manganese price strength feeding Tshipi JV cash flow
Speculative Exxaro consolidation of the Tshipi structure
Continued high dividend payout given zero corporate debt
risks: Manganese price spike partly tied to transient Middle East supply disruption, could fade
5-year earnings decline trend (-10% annually)
Recent insider selling
Takeover speculation unconfirmed/forum-sourced

evidence.note: Earnings A$9.45M, FCF positive A$4.47M, no debt, but earnings declined 10%/yr over 5 years; notable recent insider selling; P/E 12.8x vs market 17.1x.

tickers.thesis: POSCO Holdings' July CEO Investor Day laid out a KRW16.7 trillion (2026-2028) growth investment plan and a 'Triple-Core Portfolio' (Industrial Resources, Strategic Resources, Energy Resources), including expanding overseas steel capacity to 10Mtpa by 2031 (India, US, Indonesia) and optimizing listed-subsidiary stakes toward a 1.0x PBR target by 2028. This supports the Steel LONG thesis with added optionality from lithium/resource diversification and shareholder-friendly capital allocation (buybacks funded by stake sales).
catalysts: KRW16.7tn 2026-2028 growth capex and Triple-Core Portfolio execution
Overseas steel capacity expansion to 10Mtpa by 2031 in high-growth markets
Stake optimization + 10% of proceeds to buybacks targeting PBR re-rating to 1.0x by 2028
risks: China steel oversupply and export competition pressuring margins
Execution/capital-allocation risk diluting focus as lithium/energy pivot accelerates
Holding company discount could persist despite restructuring

evidence.note: CEO Investor Day: KRW16.7tn growth investment 2026-2028, stake optimization to ~50%, 10% of proceeds to buybacks

tickers.thesis: Triple Flag's Q2 2026 preliminary results beat expectations, with $129.2 million revenue (versus $124.2 million FactSet consensus) from 28,674 gold-equivalent ounces, and management reaffirmed it is on track for its recently increased 2026 GEO guidance of 100,000-110,000 ounces, all while completing a $440 million Ravenswood gold stream acquisition in Australia and buying back $20 million of stock with over $1 billion of available liquidity. As a royalty/streaming model with no direct operating cost exposure, this is the type of low-cost, defensive precious-metals exposure the commodity backdrop favors even in a short-to-medium-term gold environment, and the Ravenswood accretive deal plus buybacks signal management confidence in deploying its liquidity.
catalysts: Ravenswood stream cash flow contribution ramping
Continued GEO growth toward 100,000-110,000 oz 2026 guidance
Further accretive stream/royalty acquisitions funded by $1B+ liquidity
Continued share buybacks
risks: Royalty model still ultimately leveraged to underlying gold/silver price direction
Deployment risk on further M&A at full liquidity
Counterparty/operator risk across the 242-asset portfolio, particularly development-stage royalties

evidence.source: MT Newswires - Triple Flag Precious Metals Reports Q2 Revenue; Reaffirms Guidance
evidence.note: Q2 revenue $129.2M vs $124.2M FactSet consensus from 28,674 GEOs; on track for 2026 GEO guidance of 100,000-110,000 oz

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Triple Flag Precious Metals Corp 6-K

Value-added spread normalization

Automotive/xEV demand slowdown

tickers.thesis: Iluka is scored ~16% below fair value by a Simply Wall St DCF screen (Jul 12 2026), and has landed a binding magnet rare-earth-oxide offtake with an unnamed automotive firm (2028 start, $155-172M revenue over the term) plus a long-term REO concentrate supply deal with VHM (Jul 1 2026) to feed its Eneabba refinery, which is now over 50% built with AU$1.65B in non-recourse Australian government loan financing confirmed. This gives Iluka a well-funded rare earths call option layered on top of its core titanium/mineral sands business (NEUTRAL backdrop, aero-constructive), though shares still fell 3% on the offtake news, suggesting the market wants to see revenue land before re-rating.
catalysts: Eneabba refinery construction milestones toward mid-2027 commissioning
Additional REO offtake agreements
Government loan drawdown supporting balance sheet
risks: Refinery construction/commissioning execution risk
Offtake revenue doesn't start until 2028
Mineral sands (titanium) pricing remains base-case NEUTRAL, not a strong standalone catalyst

evidence.note: Iluka ~16% below fair value following rare earths focus
Binding magnet REO offtake with automotive firm ($155-172M revenue); AU$1.65B government loan confirmed for Eneabba refinery (>50% complete)
Long-term REO concentrate supply deal with VHM (Goschen deposit) feeding Eneabba

tickers.thesis: Bigdata coverage on REXC itself is limited to index/ETF-mechanics news rather than performance commentary - notably that Nasdaq launched the underlying Nasdaq Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China Capped Index effective Jul 7 2026, plus general fund-prospectus risk language (GraniteShares 2x REMX filing, Jul 9 2026) noting rare earth prices are 'subject to significant volatility' and concentrated Chinese supply can cause 'severe price dislocations.' As a basket vehicle explicitly targeting non-China rare earth producers, REXC is a structurally clean way to express the rare earths LONG thesis (NdPr +74% YoY) even though no entity-specific earnings/flow data surfaced.
catalysts: Continued NdPr/rare earth price strength
Index rebalancing/inflows as ex-China supply chain theme gains investor attention
China export policy tightening driving ex-China premium
risks: No entity-specific (holdings-level) performance data found in this search
Underlying holdings' individual execution risk (many are early-stage developers)
High volatility inherent to thematic/leveraged-adjacent rare earth ETFs

evidence.note: Nasdaq Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China Capped Index launched effective Jul 7 2026
Rare earth/strategic metals prices flagged as subject to severe volatility and China-concentration risk

tickers.thesis: Arafura made a final investment decision for its $1.6bn Nolans rare earths project (4,440t/year NdPr oxide, Australia's first fully integrated ore-to-oxide operation) and signed a binding term sheet with an Indian industrial group for 500tpa of NdPr magnet-feed offtake over 5+ years — concrete project advancement aligned with the desk's rare earths LONG thesis (NdPr +74% YoY). The project is now substantially financed via a completed A$350M placement plus a A$25M share purchase plan, though this comes with meaningful near-term dilution (over 1.3bn new shares issued across two tranches).
catalysts: Nolans construction start (September 2026)
Further offtake agreements following the Indian term sheet
NdPr price momentum (+74% YoY per desk backdrop)
risks: Substantial dilution from A$350M+A$25M capital raise (>1.3bn new shares)
Construction/execution risk on a first-of-kind integrated project
Foreign ASX listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: FID approved for $1.6bn Nolans project; to deliver 4,440t/year NdPr oxide, Australia's third-largest rare earths producer
Binding term sheet with Indian firm for 500tpa NdPr magnet feed offtake, 5+2 year term
Completed Tranche 2 of A$350M placement, raising ~A$174.5M; SPP closed July 7, results due July 13

analyst.note: Baowu deputy GM Hou Angui: 'steel industry will face severe challenges in the next five to ten years'

tickers.thesis: Baoshan (Baowu) remains China's most profitable listed steelmaker — FY2025 net profit rebounded 40.5% to RMB10.3bn and it stayed profitable (RMB2.2bn) in Q1 2026 even as the broader domestic steel sector posted losses — but a leadership change (Zou Jixin resigning after 7.5 years, Group Chairman Hu Wangming stepping in) and persistently weak Chinese steel demand/pricing sit awkwardly against the desk's LONG steel thesis, which is anchored in US HRC tightness rather than China. Exposure here is effectively a bet on China domestic steel bottoming, which Mysteel expects to be a weak H2 recovery at best.
catalysts: Mysteel-flagged weak H2 2026 construction steel price recovery
Baowu decarbonization/AI initiatives, Rio Tinto Pilbara DRI trials
Carbon market allowance allocation for steel sector (April 2026)
risks: Weak China steel demand/pricing vs desk's US-HRC-driven long thesis
Leadership transition uncertainty
Foreign China A-share listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: FY2025 net profit RMB10.35bn (industry-leading); Q1 2026 net profit RMB2.225bn despite sector-wide Q1 losses; CEO transition to Hu Wangming
Domestic construction steel prices expected to weakly bottom and rise only in H2 2026; Baowu deputy GM warns of severe multi-year industry challenges
Baowu/Rio Tinto complete Pilbara Blend DRI pelletisation trials at Baoshan Zhanjiang, part of decarbonization partnership

tickers.thesis: Royal Gold posted record Q1 2026 results (revenue $469.1M, +142.5% YoY; 83% adjusted EBITDA margin) on record realized gold ($4,873/oz) and silver ($84.33/oz) prices plus the first full-quarter contribution from the Sandstorm Gold and Horizon Copper acquisitions, consolidating the mid-tier royalty space. RBC trimmed its PT slightly to $305 (from $310) but maintains Outperform, and consensus remains Overweight — consistent with the desk's view that royalty names deserve a higher score than operating miners even with gold SHORT-medium overall.
catalysts: 2026 guidance tracking within range (gold 290-320koz, silver 3.0-3.5Moz, copper 21-25M lbs) through Q1
Hod Maden project restructuring expected to close H2 2026, preserving ~4% NAV exposure with reduced capex risk
Full integration of Sandstorm/Horizon Copper deals expanding royalty base
risks: Gold desk view is SHORT-medium; elevated realized prices ($4,873/oz) create high bar for continued outperformance
RGLD down 5.4% since last earnings report despite record results, signaling market skepticism
Turkish regulatory approval still pending for Hod Maden restructuring

evidence.note: Q1 2026 revenue $469.1M (+142.5% YoY), record operating cash flow $293.6M, net income $281.1M, EBITDA margin 83%

tickers.thesis: Australia's flagship pure-play spodumene producer (om 9/10 — every price move flows to the bottom line) guiding to higher FY27 production at lower unit costs after completing the Pilgangoora expansion, with an idle circuit as free optionality and a new mid-stream facility commissioning. The -21.3% vs 50DMA pullback maps exactly to the Jianxiawo-restart supply scare, yet spodumene held up better than China chemicals — physical concentrate market still tight. Best-quality way to own the lithium NEUTRAL-two-sided call; buy weakness only if carbonate stabilizes.
catalysts: July quarterly: shipped volumes, realised spodumene price, unit costs
possible restart of idled processing circuit
Q3 restock / DoD stockpile tender supporting sentiment
risks: Jianxiawo + West Africa supply returning
spodumene-carbonate spread normalization compressing realized prices
AUD/liquidity (not IBKR-tradable in book)

evidence.note: Guides stronger spodumene output with continued unit-cost declines; Pilgangoora expansion complete; idle circuit under restart review
Chinese restart approval injected supply uncertainty; China lithium chemicals softened but spodumene pricing 'comparatively resilient'; mid-stream facility commissioning
score 9/10 — Pure-play; every price move flows to bottom line

tickers.thesis: Anglo American Platinum (now demerged as Valterra Platinum) is our highest-conviction PGM short, but recent evidence cuts against that stance near-term: Berenberg upgraded Valterra to Buy from Hold on June 19, 2026, raising price targets to £71/R1,550, calling shareholder returns an 'immediate priority' and describing itself as 'constructive on the PGM outlook.' We flag this as a genuine risk to the short thesis and will watch the Q2 production report (July 17) and H1 results (July 29) closely, alongside Berenberg's forecast of H1 revenue and EBITDA below Visible Alpha consensus (ZAR71.6bn vs ZAR78.2bn revenue).
catalysts: Q2 2026 production results July 17, 2026
H1 2026 interim results July 29, 2026
Potential capital returns given company's 'immediate priority' framing per Berenberg
risks: Berenberg upgrade and constructive PGM outlook commentary directly conflict with our SHORT-high positioning
Production shifted to Q3 to manage winter power levies could distort H1/Q3 comparisons
Berenberg's own H1 estimates sit below consensus, suggesting downside surprise risk despite the upgrade

evidence.note: Berenberg upgrades Valterra Platinum to Buy, PT £71/R1,550, cites constructive PGM outlook and shareholder-return priority; Q2 production July 17, H1 results July 29
Anglo Platinum demerger to Valterra Platinum completed May 2025, part of Anglo's copper-focused simplification post-Teck merger approval

tickers.thesis: RBC raised its long-term uranium price forecast to $110/lb from $100/lb and lifted its Cameco price target to C$175 from C$160 (Outperform) citing sovereign and utility demand and contract settlement prices above term prices, while Q1 2026 uranium segment adjusted EBITDA rose to $423M from $286M YoY and 2026 attributable production guidance of 19.5-21.5M lbs was maintained even through the Cigar Lake suspension and earlier McArthur River/Key Lake flooding disruptions; the uranium demand backdrop (nuclear/AI power) is constructive, but CCJ already trades at 93.5x trailing earnings, so valuation caps the attractiveness despite strong fundamentals.
catalysts: RBC raised long-term uranium price forecast to $110/lb and price target to C$175
Sustained sovereign/utility uranium demand tied to new reactor projects
Westinghouse stake providing broader nuclear value-chain exposure beyond mining
risks: Stock trades at 93.5x trailing earnings, pricing in significant optimism
Second operational disruption this year (Cigar Lake suspension, following McArthur River/Key Lake flooding)
Uranium sector still 10-30% below moving averages despite the demand narrative

evidence.note: RBC raises long-term uranium price forecast to $110/lb from $100/lb, raises CCJ target to C$175 from C$160, Outperform
Q1 2026 net earnings $131M, adjusted EBITDA $509M, uranium segment EBITDA $423M vs $286M YoY; valuation at 93.5x trailing earnings flagged as main risk
Cigar Lake suspension noted as second 2026 operational disruption after McArthur River/Key Lake flooding; 2026 guidance of 19.5-21.5M lbs attributable maintained

tickers.thesis: Korea Zinc's core zinc/lead/tin franchise sits within the book's LONG zinc/tin thesis, and the company has been building critical-minerals optionality via its strategic partnership and $85.2M equity investment in TMC (deep-sea nodule processing/pCAM refining technology). The bigger near-term overhang is governance: the prolonged MBK Partners/Young Poong hostile takeover battle continues, with shares falling 4% in late June amid labor union pushback, and as a Korea Exchange-listed name it carries real IBKR tradability/access constraints for foreign investors.
catalysts: Zinc/lead/tin backdrop rated LONG by the book
Strategic partnership with TMC for pCAM/non-ferrous refining technology, diversifying into battery materials
Potential resolution of MBK control battle could remove an overhang on the stock
risks: Prolonged MBK Partners hostile takeover/control battle; shares fell 4% on renewed tension (2026-06-30)
Korea Exchange listing - IBKR tradability/foreign access risk
Labor union opposition adds governance and execution uncertainty

evidence.note: Korea Zinc shares fell 4% amid renewed MBK Partners takeover battle; labor union opposition to MBK control
Korea Zinc holds $85.2M equity stake plus warrants in TMC, partnering on non-ferrous refining/pCAM technology for nickel-copper-cobalt processing

tickers.thesis: NexGen's Q1 2026 call and subsequent filings confirm the defining catalyst has landed: the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission issued final federal approval (license to prepare site and construct) for the Rook I uranium project just 14 business days after hearings concluded, clearing the way for full-scale construction alongside continued exploration success at Patterson Corridor East. The company remains pre-production and pre-revenue, so financing and construction execution over the next several years are now the key swing factors, but the regulatory de-risking plus uranium sales contracts structured for future spot-price upside give NXE strong leverage to a constructive uranium backdrop.
catalysts: Full-scale Rook I construction start following CNSC approval
Continued Athabasca Basin exploration success (Patterson Corridor East)
Uranium sales contracts with spot-price-linked upside
risks: Pre-revenue, multi-year construction and financing execution risk
Uranium price volatility ahead of first production
Large capex requirements for the Rook I build

evidence.source: Nasdaq - NexGen Energy Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
evidence.note: CEO Leigh Curyer confirmed CNSC issued license to prepare site and construct Rook I 14 business days after hearings concluded; called it the defining milestone

tickers.thesis: CSN (National Steel) posted Q1 2026 consolidated EBITDA up ~5.5-5.6% YoY with 1.8pt margin expansion, helped by diversification into cement and logistics offsetting weaker steel/mining, and reduced leverage to 3.36x from 3.47x. This supports our Steel LONG bias, but Moody's-linked commentary flags leverage staying elevated (5.5x-6.5x over 12-18 months) on lower steel and iron ore prices, with 2026 iron ore production/shipments expected roughly flat to down ~1.5Mt in H1, capping near-term upside absent asset sales or accelerated deleveraging.
catalysts: Further deleveraging via debt reduction or asset sales
Iron ore price recovery toward $80-100/t (61% Fe) scenario
Continued cement/logistics diversification offsetting steel weakness
risks: Elevated leverage (5.8x Moody's-adjusted) limits credit upgrade path
Steel import pressure and BRL strength cited as 2026 headwinds
Flat-to-declining iron ore shipments in H1 2026

evidence.note: Q1 2026 EBITDA +5.5-5.6% YoY, leverage down to 3.36x from 3.47x, diversified portfolio credited
Adjusted EBITDA ~BRL8.8bn TTM to March 2026, leverage 5.8x; expected to stay 5.5x-6.5x on lower steel/iron ore prices
CSN 2026 iron ore production seen flat vs 2025; H1 2026 shipments to fall ~1.5Mt

risks: Silver price backdrop short-low after halving from January highs

Copper production declined 11% YoY on ore-processing mix shift

tickers.thesis: Elementos' Oropesa tin project in Spain was granted 'Overriding Public Interest' classification by the Andalucía regional government (Jun 23 2026), formally recognizing tin as a national priority raw material and de-risking permitting (including water licenses) - shares jumped ~10% on the news, a concrete de-risking catalyst consistent with the tin LONG backdrop. The company remains pre-production (no earnings/production data surfaced), and recent disclosures are mostly incentive-option administrivia, so this is a speculative, single-project development story rather than a name with current cash-flow leverage to the tin price.
catalysts: Water license and remaining permit approvals following OPI classification
Project financing/offtake agreements
Continued tin price strength (LONG backdrop)
risks: Still pre-production; no revenue/earnings base
Financing/dilution risk ahead of construction
Spain permitting timeline despite OPI classification

evidence.note: Oropesa tin project granted Overriding Public Interest classification in Spain; shares up ~10%
Director option exercises/lapses around Jun 30 2026 - routine capital structure activity, no operational catalyst

tickers.thesis: Empire Metals finalized an integrated metallurgical flowsheet at its Pitfield titanium project in Western Australia, achieving 98% titanium extraction and a 99% TiO2 pigment product in bench-scale testwork, with piloting starting Q3 2026 and feasibility studies in Q4. This is purely development-stage progress with an updated mineral resource estimate due July/August 2026 -- consistent with a neutral stance, as there is no production, revenue, or titanium price exposure yet, though technical de-risking is proceeding on schedule.

Continuous metallurgical piloting starting Q3 2026
Feasibility studies in Q4 2026
risks: Pre-revenue explorer with no near-term cash flow
Piloting/feasibility results could still reveal cost or scale-up challenges
Reference industry production costs ($1,300-3,300/t for rutile pigment routes) frame a wide potential cost outcome

evidence.note: Integrated metallurgical flowsheet finalized: 99% TiO2 pigment achievable, 98% titanium extraction, high-grade alumina co-product; industry rutile pigment costs $1,300-3,300/t.
Continuous metallurgical piloting to start Q3 2026 to validate design criteria ahead of feasibility studies.

Continued net losses, though narrowing YoY

analyst.Jefferies: Hold, PT drifted from AU$1.20 (May) to AU$1.05 (Jul) amid Indonesian nickel policy risk

tickers.thesis: Jefferies (May 25, Jun 17, Jul 6 2026 notes) maintains a Hold on Nickel Industries, warning the new Indonesian HPM benchmark price formula could push RKEF operations 'loss-making' and raise HPAL cash costs ~40% industry-wide, even as it holds a Buy-then-Hold rating with price targets moved from AU$1.20 to AU$1.05 to AU$1.00 across the window - a downward drift. Q2 adjusted core profit of $80M and ongoing HPAL stake deals (TMI, CNE, Sampala) show operational progress, but Jefferies explicitly flags 'Indonesia's capricious policy regime' warrants caution, squarely matching the NEUTRAL-bearish nickel backdrop.
catalysts: ENC nickel processing facility reaching full capacity (H2 2027)
HPAL stake consolidation (TMI/CNE/Sampala) NPV accretion
Nickel price recovery / supply-led rebalancing
risks: Indonesian HPM benchmark price formula raising RKEF/HPAL costs
Policy unpredictability ('capricious policy regime' per Jefferies)
Operational downtime (Hengjaya, ANI/ONI maintenance)

evidence.note: New HPM price formula could swing RKEF ops loss-making; Jefferies Buy, AU$1.20 PT at the time
Q2 adjusted core profit $80M; Jefferies Hold, AU$1.00 PT

tickers.thesis: World's largest cobalt refiner with an integrated DRC-Indonesia-cathode chain, screening cheapest in its cohort (~12x rolling P/E) with >33% share in 9-series ultra-high-nickel cathode and institutional targets >50% above spot — but the stock has round-tripped (-24% vs 50DMA, -34.6% vs 200DMA) as the July battery-chain unwind erased a June cobalt-driven pop. DFS economics remain unchanged from the April 2026 announcement.

risks: Loss of Rio Tinto as project operator (fundamentals unchanged per Rio, but a governance/financing overhang)
Early-stage development company, no revenue
Foreign ASX/LSE listing — IBKR tradability risk

tickers.thesis: Perpetua's Stibnite Gold Project is the only identified domestic US antimony reserve and just secured full construction financing: the Export-Import Bank's board unanimously approved a $2.9 billion senior secured loan in May 2026 under the Make More in America initiative, which combined with cash on hand is expected to fully fund construction, and a federal court in Idaho denied opponents' bid to halt work, clearing crews to begin building the access road. With roughly 4.8 million ounces of gold reserves, a ~300,000 oz/year gold production profile over a 15-year life, and direct DoD-relevant antimony supply for ammunition and missile components amid China/Russia/Tajikistan sourcing dependence, this aligns squarely with the antimony-long, China-export-control backdrop.
catalysts: EXIM $2.9B loan-backed construction now underway (access road started)
First antimony production positioning against DoD/defense demand
~300,000 oz/year gold production profile once operational
Continued legal/permitting clearance following denied injunction
risks: Pre-revenue, multi-year construction execution risk despite financing now secured
Large project capex and construction timeline risk typical of mine builds
Potential for further legal challenges from project opponents

evidence.source: Associated Press - The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
evidence.note: EXIM Bank board unanimously approved $2.9B senior secured loan in May 2026 to fully fund Stibnite Gold Project construction; Idaho federal court denied injunction, access road construction began

tickers.thesis: UBS initiated Lundin Gold at Buy with a C$103 target (May 28 2026), citing AISC below $1,200/oz, ~$1B/yr FCF at spot, and a 'world-class' Fruta del Norte underground mine - the low-cost profile that the gold SHORT-medium backdrop explicitly favors even amid broader caution on the metal. Continued high realized gold prices
risks: Single-asset (Fruta del Norte) concentration
Rising AISC from royalty and statutory profit-sharing as gold price climbs
Ecuador country/political risk

tickers.thesis: Recent sell-side commentary cuts against a naive short: AlphaValue/Baader raised its Norsk Hydro price target on May 13, 2026, noting the stock (+20% since late February) has been a top beneficiary of Iran-war-driven aluminum price and energy-cost dynamics, and Arctic Securities upgraded shares to Hold from Sell on July 1, 2026, both citing that Hydro's integrated power-plus-alumina structure cushions it versus pure-play smelters. That integration is exactly why this remains a lower-conviction short rather than a high-conviction one: Hydro is less exposed to a pure aluminum price decline than the MetalMiner-flagged LME weakness would suggest, and continued capex (new NOK1.1bn Karmoy wire rod line with Skanska) signals management sees durable demand, so the short thesis rests more on OTC/ADR liquidity and eventual aluminum price mean-reversion than on company-specific deterioration.
catalysts: LME aluminum price normalization lower as Middle East supply (Qatalum) restores
Broader base metals demand softening
Potential reversal of the Iran-war risk premium currently supporting the shares
risks: Integrated power+alumina model structurally cushions Hydro versus pure smelters
Sell-side sentiment has turned more constructive (Hold upgrade, raised PT) in the past two months
OTC ADR (NHYDY) carries wider spreads/lower liquidity than the Oslo-listed NHY.OL line, complicating short execution and hedging via IBKR

evidence.note: Raised Norsk Hydro price target to NOK127, citing +20% share gain since late Feb 2026 from Iran-war aluminum price rally and Qatalum-linked energy dynamics; lifted 2026 EPS estimate after Q1 profit beat
Upgraded Norsk Hydro to Hold from Sell, price target kept at NOK90

tickers.thesis: IperionX's Titan project DFS returned a strong $813M after-tax NPV (39% IRR, 3.6-year payback) and the company secured $6.6M in US Department of Defense funding to expand titanium production for defense applications — consistent with the desk's constructive aero/defense view on IperionX specifically within an otherwise NEUTRAL titanium stance. However, a $50M ADS public offering priced at $21.98 sent shares down over 20% in a single session, and analysts forecast revenue to stay small in 2026 ($2.25M) before scaling to $45.84M (2027) and $118.32M (2028), with profitability not expected until 2028.

risks: $50M ADS offering caused >20% single-day share drop (dilution)

tickers.thesis: Rusal remains loss-making and has not paid dividends since H1 2022, with no plans to resume for Q1 2026, at a time when LME aluminum has fallen ~15.5% in June to ~$3,112 and buyers are told to purchase only as needed. Simply Wall St's DCF puts fair value near HK$0.32 versus a HK$3.1 share price, implying the stock is trading well above intrinsic cash-flow value even as the company keeps investing capex in digital-twin refinery upgrades rather than returning cash.

Digital twin rollout across alumina refineries improving unit costs by 2028
risks: Western sanctions on Russian entities make this effectively untradeable/high-risk for a US-based book
No dividend visibility while metallurgy sector-wide (NLMK, MMK) also suspends payouts
Aluminum backdrop is SHORT into a falling LME market
Foreign listing (HKEX) with limited IBKR tradability given sanctions exposure

Rusal deploying 100+ digital twins across alumina refineries by 2028 to improve process efficiency
DCF fair value ~HK$0.32 vs HK$3.1 trading price; company currently loss-making with earnings under pressure

tickers.thesis: Orla's July 9, 2026 operating update shows 169,471 ounces of gold produced in H1 2026, keeping the company on track for full-year guidance of 340,000-360,000 ounces at AISC of $1,550-1,750/oz, with outperformance driven by Musselwhite and Camino Rojo back to normal after a brief interruption. The pending Equinox Gold combination is set to close in Q3 2026, adding scale and diversification, while South Railroad permitting is an additional second-half catalyst, making this a rare combination of operational execution plus a near-term M&A re-rating catalyst in a still-supportive gold price environment.
catalysts: Equinox Gold combination closing Q3 2026
South Railroad final permits
Continued Musselwhite outperformance versus guidance
August 4, 2026 Q2 financial results release
risks: Integration risk from the Equinox Gold combination
AISC guidance of $1,550-1,750/oz is mid-cost relative to peers, so margin is sensitive to gold price pullbacks
Special shareholder meeting (July 22, 2026) execution/approval risk for the transaction

tickers.thesis: Endeavour Mining trades as a high-beta proxy on gold, which has been volatile and richly valued relative to Barclays' estimated $1,300/oz fair value (gold has fallen 'nearly a quarter' since the Mideast conflict escalated to ~$4,135/oz, still far above fair value); the stock fell 5.2% on a weak-metals day and rallied 4.2% on gold strength, confirming tight correlation. This directly supports the desk's gold SHORT-med view — Endeavour offers leveraged downside if gold mean-reverts toward Barclays' fair-value estimate, despite Barclays itself listing it among recommended gold miners on a 2026/27 price-forecast basis ($1,479/$1,500).
catalysts: Barclays 2026/27 gold price forecasts ($1,479/$1,500/oz)
Potential further Mideast de-escalation reducing safe-haven bid
risks: High beta to gold price both ways
Barclays fair-value estimate ($1,300) implies material downside from spot
Foreign TSX listing — IBKR tradability risk (US listing may also exist)

evidence.note: Endeavour Mining down 3.2% on weak metals prices day (gold $4,134.67/oz)

tickers.thesis: GLD tracks spot gold, which has pulled back sharply from its January 2026 all-time high above $5,300/oz to around $4,100 by July 1 - a fourth consecutive monthly decline including a 10% drop in June - consistent with our gold SHORT-medium bias even as central banks remain structural buyers (474t purchased in Q1 2026, second-highest quarterly total on record, with ~90% of central banks planning further additions). The 12-month total return of +22.27% masks a deteriorating recent trend that argues for caution on fresh long exposure at these levels.

Central bank buying providing a longer-term floor
Gold-silver ratio dynamics
risks: Continued near-term downside momentum (4 straight monthly declines)
Real yields backing up further, pressuring non-yielding bullion
Reversal risk if safe-haven demand resurges on macro shocks

tickers.thesis: Eramet remains consistent with our Nickel NEUTRAL stance: nickel prices near $19,000/t are supportive, but the company hasn't reached full nameplate capacity at its Argentina nickel site and its Indonesian nickel ore mining permits are now exhausted, limiting its ability to capitalize on H2 strength, while manganese prices are softening after a strong start to the year. Oddo BHF maintained a Neutral rating (PT raised to €63) but flagged the stock as 'largely uninvestable' pending clarity on a €500M capital increase, high leverage (ND/EBITDA >5x), and governance uncertainty (interim CEO, rumored Duval family exit).

Resolution of €500M capital increase and CEO/governance overhang
risks: ND/EBITDA leverage above 5x
Governance uncertainty (interim CEO, capital increase terms unresolved)
Nickel ore permit exhaustion in Indonesia capping upside capture
