However, the balance sheet remains thin ($7.5M working capital), the company continues to post multi-year net losses (~$22.6M in 2025), and it carries incremental credit exposure (~$4.1M) to Lion Energy financing - keeping this a speculative, capital-constrained story consistent with a neutral manganese stance rather than a clear directional call.
catalysts: Continued scale-up of AquaRefining platform toward commercial deployment
2026 Annual Meeting August 18, 2026
Improving lithium/battery materials market conditions
risks: Thin liquidity ($7.5M working capital) relative to commercialization capex needs
Lion Energy financing exposure (~$4.1M)
Continued net losses; phased/capital-efficient approach implies slow scale-up

Lithium/battery materials market improving in 2026; phased, capital-efficient commercialization approach
Net loss $(22.6)M in 2025 vs $(24.6)M in 2024; TSR declined 8% 2024-2025

Riley Securities initiated coverage of NioCorp with a Buy rating and $12 price target on June 9, 2026, calling the Elk Creek niobium/scandium/rare-earth project one of the more advanced, lower-risk critical minerals developments in the US, and CEO Mark Smith has been publicly amplifying the China heavy rare earth export-control narrative (Fox News op-ed, June 1) that underpins the long-term demand case. tickers.thesis: United States Antimony's Q1 2026 results missed badly, with revenue of $6.8 million versus $14.8 million expected and a wider-than-expected loss, but the company reiterated its full-year 2026 gross revenue guidance of $125 million, implying a large ramp is still expected in coming quarters. The bigger structural tailwind is policy-driven: the U.S. Department of War announced a $27 million Defense Production Act Title III investment in USAC in March 2026 to expand Montana processing and develop Alaska antimony mining, directly aligned with the antimony-long, China-export-control backdrop, though the near-term revenue miss and guidance-dependent ramp keep this a show-me story until quarterly execution catches up to the policy narrative.
catalysts: $27M DPA Title III-funded Montana processing/Alaska mining expansion
Ramp toward reiterated $125M FY2026 revenue guidance
Continued antimony price strength from China export controls and DoD demand
risks: Q1 2026 revenue miss ($6.8M vs $14.8M expected) and wider-than-expected loss signal execution/ramp risk
Guidance reiteration requires a significant back-half acceleration to be met
Company diversifying into cobalt, gold, tungsten, zeolite could dilute antimony-pure-play thesis

evidence.source: MT Newswires - United States Antimony Reports Q1 Loss, Revenue Falls
evidence.note: Q1 revenue $6.8M vs $14.8M expected, loss of $0.08/share vs breakeven year-ago; reiterated 2026 gross revenue guidance of $125M

evidence.source: Associated Press - Antimony Market to Reach 140.27 Kilotons by 2031
evidence.note: US Department of War announced $27M DPA Title III investment in USAC (March 2026) to expand Montana processing and Alaska antimony mining

tickers.thesis: Uranium Energy Corp hit real production milestones in its fiscal Q3 2026 (ended April 30, 2026), commencing output at Burke Hollow -- the largest US greenfield ISR project to enter production in over a decade -- and starting new header houses at Christensen Ranch, while a cited UxC 2026 Q1 outlook projects a structural 2026-2028 supply/demand gap of about 65 million pounds U3O8 supporting the long-term bull case. Near-term financials are still rough, however: the company recorded no uranium sales in the quarter, produced only 32,195 pounds at a total cost of $54.61/lb, and posted a net loss, so this remains a production-ramp story where execution needs to catch up to the constructive structural uranium narrative.
catalysts: Full-quarter production contribution from Christensen Ranch and Burke Hollow in fiscal Q4 2026
Declining unit costs as ISR volumes scale
Structural uranium supply deficit (UxC-cited ~65M lb gap 2026-2028) supporting prices
1.456 million pounds of purchased uranium inventory providing sales optionality
risks: No uranium sales recorded in fiscal Q3 2026 despite active mine development spend
High current unit production cost ($54.61/lb total, $46.69/lb cash) versus scale needed
Net loss and rising cash used in operations ($90M vs $41M prior year) during ramp phase

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Uranium Energy Corp 8-K
evidence.note: CEO Amir Adnani confirmed production start at Burke Hollow and new header houses at Christensen Ranch

evidence.source: Nasdaq - Why Is Uranium Energy (UEC) Up 5.4% Since Last Earnings Report?
evidence.note: Q3 FY2026: no uranium sales recorded, 32,195 lbs produced at $54.61/lb total cost, net cash used in operations $90M vs $41M prior year

tickers.thesis: Ramaco's Q1 2026 call highlighted an estimated 2 million tons of domestic met coal production already removed from the market in 2025 with another 3 million tons expected in 2026, a supply-side dynamic management hopes will support pricing, while the company ended Q1 with $490 million in liquidity, up 310% year-over-year, funding both a coal capacity expansion (Laurel Fork restart, Berwind third section) and continued buybacks. The rare-earth optionality at Brook Mine is real but still pre-revenue, and management itself said the stock reflects little value for it yet, so the near-term thesis is a coal-driven balance-sheet and supply-tightening story with REE as a free call option.
catalysts: Continued domestic met coal supply cuts tightening pricing
Berwind and Laurel Fork production ramp adding 100k-200k tons low-vol coal in 2026
Brook Mine rare earth project advancement
Ongoing share buybacks with $63M of authorization remaining
risks: Core revenue still tied to cyclical metallurgical coal pricing, not rare earths
Brook Mine REE value not yet reflected or monetized
Execution risk on new rail loadout and mine restarts

evidence.source: PubT - Ramaco Resources Q1 2026 Earnings Call
evidence.note: Management cites ~2M tons of 2025 domestic coal supply cuts and expects 3M+ more in 2026 to support pricing; $490M liquidity up 310% YoY

tickers.thesis: Ivanhoe Electric's preliminary feasibility study shows meaningful leverage to copper prices — $1.4B after-tax NPV8% and 20% IRR at $4.25/lb copper, with NPV rising $240M per $0.25/lb increase in copper price — directly aligned with the long-copper backdrop (tariff catalyst), and the company just amended its Saudi Arabian JV with Maaden to extend the operational timeline through July 2033 and grant more operating flexibility; this remains a pre-production developer, so the copper price leverage is a multi-year call rather than a near-term earnings driver, and financing/execution risk should be flagged.
catalysts: Amended Maaden JV extending exploration/operational timeline through July 2033 with greater technical committee authority
Significant NPV leverage to copper price ($240M per $0.25/lb) underpinning long-copper thesis
Tunnel Boring Machine incorporation updating the 2025 feasibility study
risks: Pre-production developer with $1.24B initial capex requirement still to be financed
JV amendment triggered a 1.6% premarket share price decline
Copper price assumption ($4.25/lb) below the current spot backdrop level cited, but downside sensitivity still material

evidence.note: Ivanhoe Electric amends Saudi Arabian Maaden JV, extends timeline through July 2033, grants technical committees more fund-reallocation authority; shares down 1.6% premarket

Operations concentrated in Mali, Philippines, Namibia carrying jurisdictional risk

tickers.thesis: Standard Lithium continues to fund its near-commercial Smackover DLE developments (South West Arkansas with partner Equinor, plus the high-grade Franklin Project in East Texas) primarily through dilutive at-the-market share issuance, raising $11.3 million gross in the June quarter alone at $3.59/share, on top of an $50 million ATM program launched in August 2025. The Definitive Feasibility Study for SWA is complete and the demonstration plant at LANXESS continues operating, which are genuine technical/de-risking milestones, but as a pre-revenue developer reliant on continued equity issuance and a still-uncertain lithium price recovery, this is a two-sided policy-optionality story rather than a clear directional call.
catalysts: SWA project construction/financing progress with Equinor JV
Franklin Project (East Texas) resource delineation and development
Continued demonstration plant validation of DLE technology at LANXESS site
Any US critical-minerals policy support for domestic lithium
risks: Continued reliance on dilutive ATM equity issuance
Pre-revenue, DLE technology and project financing execution risk
Lithium price remains well off prior cycle highs, pressuring project economics

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Standard Lithium Ltd 6-K
evidence.note: Issued 3.14M shares under ATM program in the June quarter at avg $3.59/share for $11.26M gross proceeds, continuing a $50M ATM program

tickers.thesis: Lifezone's own July 2026 6-K discloses it rightsized headcount from 153 to 93 employees during 2025 explicitly to preserve financial discipline amid cyclically low nickel prices and share price volatility, a direct admission that current nickel pricing is squeezing the Kabanga Nickel Project economics. Potential nickel price recovery from current cyclical lows
Further cost-out execution improving unit economics
risks: Cyclically low nickel prices compressing project returns
Workforce reductions signal financial strain
Development-stage project financing risk

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Lifezone Metals Ltd 6-K
evidence.note: Workforce cut from 153 (2024) to 93 (2025) explicitly to remain cost-effective amid cyclically low nickel prices and increased share price volatility

Recent rise in steel prices cited by management as encouraging

Total shipment volumes still weighed down by broader market conditions

sizingNote: NAV ~$10,950. Rules used: single name ≤ 8% NAV (~$875), commodity group ≤ 20% NAV, keep gross ≤ 75%. Current book: gross ~69%, net ~-31%.
